feeds.name: The Guardian - Man Utd
feeds.note: Domain may block WebFetch (verified Jun 2026) - skip without retry if it fails; its stories surface via Google News anyway

feeds.name: Manchester Evening News - Man Utd
feeds.note: Domain may block WebFetch (verified Jun 2026) - skip without retry if it fails; its stories surface via Google News anyway

feeds.name: Sky Sports - Man Utd news page
feeds.note: Dedicated United page - use this for United stories; the general Sky football RSS (11095) often has zero United items. Verify article dates before scoring, listing-page dates can be unreliable.

feeds.name: The Athletic - Man Utd
feeds.note: Blocks nothing via curl with a browser User-Agent (verified Jun 2026) but article bodies are paywalled - use headlines/standfirsts for discovery, then corroborate via WebSearch (Whitwell/Ornstein searches). Home of the best United beat reporting.

feeds.name: r/reddevils breaking-news radar
feeds.note: Fastest surface for breaking United news, but it is a fan aggregator - NEVER source a story from here; use it only to discover what is breaking, then verify with a tier-1 source. Fetch via curl with a browser User-Agent (Atom feed).

feeds.name: Man Utd official news
feeds.note: Blocks WebFetch (Imperva 403) but serves plain curl with a browser User-Agent (verified Jun 2026). Fetch via Bash: curl -s -A 'Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/137.0.0.0 Safari/537.36' <url> then grep href="/en/news/..." for article slugs (slug reads as headline); curl each article URL the same way and grep <title>, "description" and datePublished for details. The only OFFICIAL-status source.

tier_meaning.3: RUMOUR only, always attributed (tabloids surface via Google News)
